question_text: A student observes that ice left in a warm room gradually disappears without ever becoming a puddle of water. Which of the following best explains this observation?

options.label: A
options.text: The ice melts into water then quickly evaporates because the room is very warm

options.label: B
options.text: The ice undergoes sublimation and changes directly into water vapour without melting

options.label: C
options.text: The ice is absorbing water from the air and becoming heavier, so it appears to disappear

options.label: D
options.text: The ice changes into a different state of matter that is invisible to the human eye

explanation: Although option A is physically possible, the absence of any liquid water being observed suggests sublimation is occurring—the ice is going directly to vapour. Sublimation can happen at room temperature, especially if the air is dry.
